brazil.summary: A real policy window is taking shape: political attention is high and the field is capable, but the coalition behind implementable proposals remains fragmented.
brazil.signal: Whether legislative attention converts into an institutionally owned implementation pathway.

germany.summary: Dense expertise and strong institutions make the window actionable, although attention is increasingly shaped by implementation questions rather than agenda setting.
germany.signal: How national implementation choices translate European rules into durable oversight capacity.

india.summary: AI attention is accelerating and the expert base is substantial, but governance proposals compete with a powerful innovation and adoption agenda.
india.signal: Whether public-interest actors can attach concrete safeguards to the country’s fast-moving AI agenda.

kenya.summary: A visible community and rising public attention create potential, but limited institutional depth makes the emerging window vulnerable to external agendas.
kenya.signal: Whether local experts gain a formal route into strategy design and implementation.

mexico.summary: Relevant expertise exists, but political attention is intermittent and few actors connect technical knowledge to a credible federal policy route.
mexico.signal: Whether a policy champion and cross-party institutional home emerge around a concrete issue.
